task.title: Write a header rule to check front-matter metadata
task.shortdesc: Header rules check the YAML front-matter metadata at the top of your Markdown files. Use them to enforce required fields, expected values, date freshness, and naming patterns.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Write a markdown-validator header rule to check YAML front-matter fields: required fields, exact values, allowed value sets, and date freshness.

prereq.p: You have an existing rule set file. See Create a rule set file. Understand the rule fields before proceeding: Anatomy of a validation rule.

step.cmd: Identify the front-matter field you want to check and the editorial requirement it must satisfy.

ul.li: The ms.topic field must equal tutorial.
The ms.date field must be within the last 365 days.

step.cmd: Choose the flag that matches how you want to process the field value.

row.entry: The field exists (any value is acceptable)
The field equals a specific value
The field's date is within N days
The field matches a regular-expression pattern

step.cmd: Choose the operator and expected value.

ul.li: For exact string match: use "operation": "==" and the expected string.
For allowed-values set: use "operation": "[]" and a comma-separated list in value.
For date freshness: use "operation": "<" and the maximum age in days.

step.cmd: Write a clear, actionable mitigation message.

info.p: The mitigation message is shown directly to authors when their document fails this rule. Write it as an imperative sentence that tells them exactly what to do. Compare:

ul.li: Clear: "Set ms.topic to one of: tutorial, how-to, concept, reference."

step.cmd: Add the rule object to the header array in your rule set file.

info.codeblock: { "id": 3, "name": "ms-topic-must-be-allowed-value", "type": "header", "query": "ms.topic", "flag": "value", "operation": "[]", "value": "tutorial, how-to, concept, reference", "level": "Required", "mitigation": "Set ms.topic to one of: tutorial, how-to, concept, reference." }

step.cmd: Test the rule against a document that should pass and a document that should fail.

info.codeblock: md-validate validate passing-doc.md --rules rules/tutorial.json md-validate validate failing-doc.md --rules rules/tutorial.json
info.p: Confirm the passing document scores correctly and the failing document shows your mitigation message. For interactive testing, see Test a rule in the REPL.
